title: Antennaria carpatica
intro: Antennaria carpatica, also known as Mountain Elbow Joint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Antennaria carpatica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Antennaria carpatica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Antennaria carpatica is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Antennaria and is a species of plant. The genus Antennaria is further classified into the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species. It is a member of the flowering plant division.

benefits: Antennaria carpatica, commonly known as mountain everlasting, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. properties: Antennaria carpatica is known for its potential therapeutic properties. constituents: Antennaria carpatica contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, including carpatic acid and its derivatives, may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits. Antennaria carpatica also contains other bioactive compounds such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which may exhibit antimicrobial and free radical scavenging activities. The plant's chemical composition is still being studied, and its active constituents may have additional properties that are yet to be discovered. preparations: Antennaria carpatica is not a commonly studied plant species, and therefore, information on herbal preparations is limited. However, the plant's close relative, Antennaria dioica (also known as mountain everlasting), is used in traditional herbal medicine. Preparations from Antennaria dioica include teas and infusions made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
